title: First Rule Breaking Training-Generalization Tradeoff

content.finding: Evolutionary search discovered first plasticity rule that beats canonical on BOTH training (+7.6%) and novel (+37.6%) performance, breaking the training-generalization tradeoff observed in all prior candidates
content.evidence: Session 7 evolutionary search (8 gen, pop 15, medium eval) followed by full protocol validation. Candidate #2 (medium eval rank: 2nd) outperformed higher-ranked Candidate #1 under full protocol.
implications: Medium eval (quick/noisy) ranking is NOT reliable predictor of full protocol performance
Second-ranked rule by medium eval was actual best performer under full protocol
Training-generalization tradeoff is not fundamental - can be overcome with right parameters
Lower amplify_mult (0.387 vs 0.548-0.732 in prior rules) may be key to generalization
Lower drift_mult (0.157 vs 0.207-0.328) may reduce overfitting
Evolutionary search successfully navigated 7-dimensional parameter space
This is the first Stage 2 parameter-space success after multiple negative results
content.key_insight: The training-generalization tradeoff observed in entries 011, 024 is NOT fundamental. The right balance of amplify_mult and drift_mult allows simultaneous improvement on both metrics. This contradicts the hypothesis that Stage 2 parameter adaptation faces a frozen frame floor.
next_questions: Can further evolution improve beyond +37.6% novel gap?
What is the theoretical upper bound for parameter-only optimization?
Does Candidate #2 maintain advantage across different birth seeds?
What internal structure (alpha distribution, attention patterns) enables dual improvement?

overturns: The hypothesis that parameter-space Stage 2 faces a frozen frame floor (implied by prior tradeoff observations)
